name: Post-isotretinoin transition
dek: The skin coming off oral isotretinoin is differently dry, differently reactive, and differently sebum-suppressed than the skin that started the course. The reset routine matters as much as the course itself — and is almost never discussed at the prescribing visit.
oneliner: How to rebuild a routine after a course finishes.
bottom: The course is the headline event; the recovery routine is the part that determines whether you maintain or relapse. Six months of patience will preserve the result; two months of impatience will compromise it.

triggers.k: Persistent dryness 4–8 weeks after course end
triggers.n: Sebum production takes months to normalise. Cream-based, ceramide-rich routine continues post-course.
The barrier is still rebuilding. Reintroduce one product at a time over 4–6 weeks.
Months, not weeks, to fully resolve. Continue petrolatum lip protection through month 6.
Even a modest tretinoin can sting in this window. Wait at least 6 months before resuming.

ingredients.name: Ceramide-rich cream cleanser
ingredients.evidence: Lifetime of post-iso patients tolerate cream cleansers indefinitely. Not a temporary swap.

ingredients.name: Ceramide + cholesterol cream
ingredients.evidence: Twice-daily for at least 6 months post-course. The most consequential step.

ingredients.name: Petrolatum-based lip ointment
ingredients.evidence: Continue 6 months post-course. The lips heal slowest.

ingredients.name: Mineral SPF 50+ daily
ingredients.evidence: Skin remains UV-sensitive; PIH risk is elevated through month 6.

ingredients.name: Azelaic 10% (after month 3)
ingredients.evidence: Gentle re-introduction of pigment / anti-inflammatory work. Avoid harsher actives until month 6.

ingredients.name: AVOID retinoids × 6 months
ingredients.evidence: Universal. Starting tretinoin at month 2 is a common, painful mistake.

phases.b: Cream cleanser, ceramide cream twice daily, petrolatum lip ointment, mineral SPF. Nothing else.
Niacinamide moisturiser. Optional azelaic 10% PM if comedones return. No retinoids yet.
Reintroduce retinoid at low concentration, twice weekly. Patience pays the dividend the course earned.
